svg.text: UNPACK OUTPUT — FEED BACK TO GRAPH
open_scene, save_scene, export_alembic, whatever you need.
Pure data. No Maya session open. No disk writes.
Just a list of descriptions traveling down the graph.
Each node doesn't DO anything yet.
It just appends a description of the work.
The maya_headless node receives that list,
serialises it, spins up mayapy, and runs every action in sequence.
One node. One subprocess. Every action runs.
Inside a real, full Maya session.
the output from mayapy and feeds it back into your graph.
Extracts the result dict for that one action.
Unpacks it into typed output ports.
Each key becomes a typed output port in the node.
→ int wire to any downstream node
Real Maya data. Back in your node graph.
Wire it anywhere. Use it like any other value.
Serialise → spawn mayapy → run all → parse stdout
